_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to compare and evaluate how 'voice' as a literary device is used across different texts to evoke emotional responses and create aesthetic effects.

_source.w: We are learning to compare and evaluate how 'voice' as a literary device is used across different texts to evoke emotional responses and create aesthetic effects.
y: Voice is what makes a text come alive — it is the personality and presence that distinguishes one writer from another.
Understanding voice as a literary device helps us appreciate the artistry of literature at the highest level.
Developing our own critical understanding of voice enriches both our reading and our writing.
eg: We compare the voices in two literary texts and evaluate how each creates distinctive emotional and aesthetic effects.
We analyse how an author sustains a particular voice throughout a text and what techniques maintain it.
We discuss how voice can be character-based (a narrator's voice) or author-based (the writer's distinctive style).
We evaluate how effectively voice evokes emotional responses — empathy, discomfort, admiration, unease.
We compare how voice operates differently in prose, poetry and drama.
We experiment with voice in our own writing, attempting to create distinctive aesthetic and emotional effects.
